content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Addressed both pending amendments. (1) Replaced silent .ok()? error handling in json_fill_none (fs.rs:2289) with explicit match arms that emit tracing::warn on all three serde failure points — serialize base, serialize overlay, and deserialize merged map — so operators can distinguish serde bugs from legitimate structural merge rejections in logs. The None return path is preserved for callers that fall through to fresh writes. (2) Added forward-compatibility documentation comments on #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] for both StartJournalDetails (model.rs:357) and CompletionJournalDetails (model.rs:392), documenting that on binary rollback after newer-format entries are written, parse() returns None, journal merge is skipped, and duplicate events accumulate — data-safe but operationally surprising until the newer binary is restored.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Amendment 1: Replaced silent .ok()? patterns in json_fill_none (fs.rs:2289) with explicit match arms at all three serde failure points — serde_json::to_value(base), serde_json::to_value(overlay), and serde_json::from_value(merged) — each emitting tracing::warn with the error before returning None
2. [Completed] Amendment 2: Added forward-compatibility documentation comment on StartJournalDetails #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] in model.rs (line 357): documents that newer-format journal entries will fail to parse on binary rollback, causing parse() to return None, merge skip, and duplicate event accumulation (data-safe but operationally surprising)
3. [Completed] Amendment 2: Added matching forward-compatibility documentation comment on CompletionJournalDetails #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] in model.rs (line 392): same tradeoff documentation as StartJournalDetails
4. [Completed] Ran cargo test (682 passed, 1 pre-existing failure unrelated to changes), cargo clippy -- -D warnings (clean), cargo fmt --check (only pre-existing drift in agent_execution_test.rs)

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 682 unit tests passed, 1 pre-existing failure (checkpoint_test::worktree_adapter — missing directory, unrelated to changes)
- cargo clippy -- -D warnings: no warnings or errors
- cargo fmt --check: all changed files (fs.rs, model.rs) clean; only pre-existing formatting diffs in unrelated tests/unit/agent_execution_test.rs
- json_fill_none now logs tracing::warn with structured error field at all three serde failure points (serialize base, serialize overlay, deserialize merged), making failures distinguishable from legitimate merge rejections in operator logs
- Both StartJournalDetails and CompletionJournalDetails doc comments explain the deny_unknown_fields forward-compatibility tradeoff: binary rollback after schema evolution causes parse() to return None, merge skip, and duplicate event accumulation — data-safe but operationally surprising
- No behavioral changes to merge logic — tracing::warn is advisory only; the None return path is preserved for callers that fall through to fresh writes
- All prior round tests continue to pass: terminal_replay_after_plan_evolution_succeeds, runless_match_rejected_when_started_at_mismatches, start_replay_after_plan_evolution_does_not_conflict, legacy_open_row_without_plan_hash_not_mislabeled_after_plan_evolution, terminal_replay_legacy_row_without_plan_hash_stays_unset, terminal_runless_replay_rejected_when_started_at_mismatches, journal_merge_fills_all_optional_fields, plan_hash_auto_populated_from_snapshot, explicit_plan_hash_takes_precedence_over_snapshot, runless_start_completed_with_named_run_id, completion_with_conflicting_plan_hash_rejects_runless_match, start_journal_details_round_trips_with_model_render, completion_journal_details_round_trips_with_model_render

## Outstanding Risks

- Pre-existing formatting drift in tests/unit/agent_execution_test.rs is unrelated to these changes but will appear in cargo fmt --check across the full workspace.
- Pre-existing test failure in checkpoint_test::worktree_adapter_creates_finds_and_resets_checkpoint_commits (missing directory in test environment) is unrelated to these changes.
